input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule T.1.4.1 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.1.4.1
            TITLE: accelerator pedal

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.4.1: Accelerator Pedal
T.1.4.1 Accelerator Pedal 
a. An Accelerator Pedal must control the Powertrain output   
b. Pedal Travel is the percent of travel from a fully released position to a fully applied 
position.  0% is fully released and 100% is fully applied. 
c. The Accelerator Pedal must: 
• Return to 0% Pedal Travel when not pushed  
• Have a positive stop to prevent any cable, actuation system or sensor from damage 
or overstress

final_answer: RULE: t.1.4.1 TITLE: accelerator pedal CONTENT: rule T.1.4.1: Accelerator Pedal T.1.4.1 Accelerator Pedal a. An Accelerator Pedal must control the Powertrain output b. Pedal Travel is the percent of travel from a fully released position to a fully applied position. 0% is fully released and 100% is fully applied. c. The Accelerator Pedal must: • Return to 0% Pedal Travel when not pushed • Have a positive stop to prevent any cable, actuation system or sensor from damage or overstress
